scope: HIP/CK source-boundary sparse-MLA backward owner made closer to Triton's query-major inner schedule while keeping the final global atomic dKV add.

implemented_changes.producer: Still one CTA per query token and H64/N64 selected window, but now stages selected KV as N64 x D128 in LDS before the 16 H/N waves consume four K32 MFMA chunks. This removes the old repeated global KV load per H16 wave.
implemented_changes.epilogue: Still computes dKV contribution in the same CTA and still ends with global atomicAdd to compressed dKV. It now stages Q and grad_output as H64 x D64 in LDS per D sweep so N16 contribution waves reuse them instead of reloading per selected-column block.
implemented_changes.group_awareness: The epilogue is compressed-group addressed and head-reduced inside the CTA before the final global atomic. With the current one-token CTA, LI top-k groups are effectively unique within a token, so the next meaningful group-aware reduction must be across multiple query tokens or via a block-level/persistent producer-consumer design.

mode0_producer_only.read: Rejected versus the 1024-thread D128 producer because the two N phases cancel the occupancy benefit.

decision.read: The D128 shared-KV producer is the right direction and cuts producer-only latency about 47%, but the warp-level contribution epilogue remains the wall. Final global atomics are no longer the dominant delta in this HIP owner.
decision.next_required_step: Replace the warp-fragment contribution path with CK block GEMM or an FMHA-style block pipeline so the CTA computes N64 x D64/128 dKV contribution as a block tile, then attach the compressed-group final atomic epilogue. Cross-token group-aware reduction cannot materialize in this one-token CTA except for rare duplicate selected groups.
